title: Entry Identity, Idempotent Upsert, Revisions, and Deletion Semantics · Goroutine Forensics
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in idempotent versioned entry ingestion model and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Bind external entry identity to feed scope and format semantics, detect collisions, and retain the raw identity basis.
Insert new, ignore unchanged, version changed, and quarantine conflicting entries atomically under unique constraints.
Store meaningful normalized revisions with source fetch, field diff, timestamps, and bounded retention instead of overwriting evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Feed Go Entry Key Scope
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 7002 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove scoped identity, idempotent outcomes, revision history, tombstones, and replay convergence; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Bind external entry identity to feed scope and format semantics, detect collisions, and retain the raw identity basis.

Inspect the Feed Go Entry Key Scope trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Reingestion Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-reingestion-reconciliation-13c1jr" that uses case 9601 to prove scoped identity, idempotent outcomes, revision history, tombstones, and replay convergence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Feed Go Reingestion Reconciliation.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 9673 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove scoped identity, idempotent outcomes, revision history, tombstones, and replay convergence; untrusted XML and feed content must not cross admission or rendering boundaries; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
